label: Lever
description: Connect to Lever to read and manage candidates, requisitions, and pipeline stages from your ATS.

form.title: Connect Lever
form.subtitle: Authenticate Anton with your Lever account to access recruiting data.

methods.label: API Key
methods.description: Paste a Lever API key generated from Settings → Integrations → API credentials. Simplest and fastest setup — no OAuth app required.
methods.how_to:
## Generate a Lever API Key

Lever issues API keys from the account settings panel. You need **Super Admin** or **Admin** access to create them.

### Steps

1. Log in to Lever at [hire.lever.co](https://hire.lever.co).
2. Click your name or avatar in the top-right corner and go to **Settings**.
3. In the left sidebar, navigate to **Integrations → API credentials**.
4. Click **Generate new key**.
5. Give the key a descriptive name like `Anton`.
6. Select the scopes you need. For full read access, enable **All data (read)**. For write access (posting notes, creating opportunities), also enable **Candidates (write)**.
7. Click **Create**. Copy the key immediately — Lever shows it only once.
8. Paste the key into the **API Key** field below.

### Notes

- Lever uses HTTP Basic Auth: the API key is the username, the password is left blank. Anton handles this automatically.
- If you lose the key, revoke it from the same **API credentials** page and generate a new one.
- Sandbox environment keys are separate — use `api.sandbox.lever.co` as the base URL if testing.
- Read [Lever's API authentication docs](https://hire.lever.co/developer/documentation#authentication) for full details.

fields.name: api_key
fields.label: API Key
fields.description: Found in Lever → Settings → Integrations → API credentials. Shown only once at creation.

fields.name: environment
fields.label: Environment
fields.description: Choose Production for live data or Sandbox for testing.

methods.label: OAuth 2.0 (BYOK)
methods.description: Use a custom Lever OAuth 2.0 app for delegated access. Requires creating an OAuth client in Lever's partner portal — more setup, but allows per-user authorization flows.
methods.how_to:
## Set Up Lever OAuth 2.0

Lever's OAuth flow requires you to register an application through their partner program. This is more involved than a simple API key — use the **API Key** method unless you specifically need delegated per-user access.

### Steps

1. Apply for partner/OAuth access at [Lever's developer portal](https://hire.lever.co/developer) or contact Lever support to get OAuth credentials.
2. Once approved, you'll receive a **Client ID** and **Client Secret** for your registered application.
3. In your OAuth app settings, add a redirect URI pointing to `http://localhost` (Anton uses a local redirect).
4. Enter the **Client ID** and **Client Secret** in the fields below.
5. Click **Submit**. Anton will open Lever's authorization screen in your browser.
6. Log in and grant the requested permissions.
7. Anton will exchange the authorization code for an access token and refresh token automatically.

### Notes

- The `offline_access` scope is required to receive a refresh token for long-lived access.
- Lever's OAuth is Auth0-based; the `audience` parameter is required for the API.
- Tokens can be revoked from Lever's **Settings → Integrations** panel.

fields.name: client_id
fields.label: OAuth Client ID
fields.description: Client ID from your registered Lever OAuth application.

fields.name: client_secret
fields.label: OAuth Client Secret
fields.description: Client secret paired with your Lever OAuth Client ID.
